content: We can refactor memory-efficient Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) implementations for large-scale autonomous agents to recursively prune the agent’s own curiosity signal encoding, rather than just compressing general transition memory: only unexpected, high-learning-gain exploratory experiences are retained long-term, while trivial, already-learned novelty signals are discarded to save memory. This creates a "curiosity sharpening" effect where the agent’s intrinsic motivation becomes increasingly focused on high-value uncharted states as its scale grows.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
